message.content: Adversaries may exploit the Verclsid binary to proxy the execution of malicious code. Verclsid, the Extension CLSID Verification Host, verifies shell extensions before their use by Windows Explorer or the Windows Shell. By running Verclsid with specific parameters, attackers can execute payloads referenced by a Class ID (CLSID), potentially loading and executing COM scriptlets (SCT) from remote servers. This method can bypass application control solutions due to Verclsid being a signed and native Windows binary. The Hancitor malware has been observed using Verclsid to download and execute malicious scripts. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and restricting the execution of Verclsid, implementing strict application whitelisting policies, and employing behavioral analysis to detect abnormal usage patterns of system binaries.
